query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Abash', 'To make ashamed', 'The author hoped the new policy would abash tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abate', 'Become less intense over time', 'Over time, the community learned to abate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abbess', 'A female superior or governess of a nunnery', 'Her abbess became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abbey', 'A monastery or society of people', 'The essay highlights the abbey of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abbot', 'The superior or head of an abbey or monastery', 'Scholars noted a striking abbot between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abbreviate', 'To make shorter', 'Over time, the community learned to abbreviate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abdicate', 'To renounce one''s throne or position of power', 'Over time, the community learned to abdicate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abdomen', 'The belly/stomach', 'Her abdomen became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abduct', 'To take away secretly by force', 'Over time, the community learned to abduct its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abed', 'In bed', 'Scholars noted a striking abed between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aberrant', 'Departing from an accepted standard', 'What seemed aberrant at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aberration', 'A departure from what is normal or expected', 'Her aberration became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abet', 'To assist or encourage by aid, especially in crime', 'The author hoped the new policy would abet tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abeyance', 'Temporary inactivity', 'Scholars noted a striking abeyance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abhor', 'To regard with disgust and hatred', 'The author hoped the new policy would abhor tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abide', 'To wait for', 'Critics argued that the plan might abide the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abject', 'Extremely bad or severe; hopeless', 'What seemed abject at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abjure', 'To renounce formally a belief or claim', 'Over time, the community learned to abjure its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abnegate', 'To renounce or give up something valued', 'Critics argued that the plan might abnegate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abnormal', 'Not conforming to rule or system', 'The author hoped the new policy would abnormal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abominable', 'Very hateful, detestable', 'The passage presents a abominable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abomination', 'Anything greatly disliked or abhorred', 'Her abomination became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aboriginal', 'Original or indigenous to a place', 'Critics argued that the plan might aboriginal the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abort', 'To miscarry, to bring forth something prematurely', 'Over time, the community learned to abort its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abortive', 'Unsuccessful', 'What seemed abortive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abound', 'To be plentiful, to be very prevalent, to overflow', 'The author hoped the new policy would abound tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aboveboard', 'In open sight, without concealment, or deception', 'Scholars noted a striking aboveboard between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abrade', 'To rub or wear away', 'Critics argued that the plan might abrade the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abrasive', 'Rough enough to wear away the outer surface', 'Critics argued that the plan might abrasive the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abridge', 'To shorten in duration', 'The author hoped the new policy would abridge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abrogate', 'To repeal or do away with formally', 'The author hoped the new policy would abrogate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abscess', 'To form such a collection of pus', 'The author hoped the new policy would abscess tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abscission', 'The act of cutting off', 'Her abscission became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abscond', 'To leave hurriedly and secretly', 'The author hoped the new policy would abscond tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Absence', 'A state of being absent', 'Her absence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Absolute', 'Free from imperfection, complete', 'Her absolute became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Absolve', 'To set free from an obligation', 'Over time, the community learned to absolve its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abstain', 'To choose not to do something, especially something one might be tempted to do', 'Critics argued that the plan might abstain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abstemious', 'Not self-indulgent, especially regarding food and drink', 'The passage presents a abstemious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abstinence', 'Voluntary forbearance esp.  from indulgence of an appetite', 'The essay highlights the abstinence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abstract', 'To separate, to remove, to take away', 'Critics argued that the plan might abstract the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abstruse', 'Difficult to understand; obscure', 'Critics argued that the plan might abstruse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abundance', 'A large quantity of something', '“The farmer was pleased with the abundance of crops this season.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abundant', 'Existing or available in large quantities; plentiful', 'The essay highlights the abundant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abusive', 'Treating badly or injuriously', 'The passage presents a abusive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abut', 'To touch by means of a mutual border, to meet', 'Critics argued that the plan might abut the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abysmal', 'Extremely hopeless, bad, or severe', 'The passage presents a abysmal portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Abyss', 'An immeasurably deep gulf or great space', 'Scholars noted a striking abyss between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accede', 'To agree to a demand or request', 'Over time, the community learned to accede its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accentuate', 'To emphasize or highlight something, making it more prominent or noticeable', 'The author hoped the new policy would accentuate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
